identity.name: Dr. Rohan Mukherjee
identity.headline: Assistant Professor of Computational Neuroscience | Spiking Networks & Learning

research_statement: My research develops biologically plausible models of learning in spiking neural networks, bridging theoretical neuroscience and machine learning to understand how the brain achieves sample-efficient generalisation.

appointments.title: Assistant Professor
appointments.institution: IIT Delhi — Dept. of Computer Science
highlights: Built a research group of 5 PhD students and 2 postdocs working on biologically plausible learning.
Raised ₹1.2 crore in competitive funding and published 3 first-author papers in the first three years.

appointments.title: Postdoctoral Fellow
highlights: Developed local plasticity rules that approximate backpropagation, published at NeurIPS 2023.

achievements: Published 3 first-author papers in Nature Machine Intelligence, NeurIPS and eLife since 2022.
Secured a ₹1.2 crore SERB core research grant and a Wellcome Trust early-career fellowship.
Built a lab of 5 PhD students and 2 postdocs from a standing start in 2021.
Mentored 200+ graduate students across two ML and neural-computation courses.
Presented invited talks at Cosyne and a NeurIPS workshop in 2023 and 2024.

publications.authors: Mukherjee R., Chen L., Rao S.
publications.title: Sample-efficient credit assignment in spiking networks
A dynamical systems view of working memory
